titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/159

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=437 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:20:37.919935+00 | translation moa=438 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:20:42.322925+00

div.p:
Omnibus Sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis presentibus et futuris Patricius de Ridale Salutem . Sciatis me concessisse et hac
mea carta confirmasse deo et Ecclesie Sancte Marie de Melros et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus donationem
Gaufridi filii Walleui de lillesclif quam fecit antedicte Ecclesie et Monachis de quattuor Bouatis terre arabi
lis interritorio de Wittun in liberam . puram . et perpetuam Elemosinam . sicut iacent in uno tenemento desuper
Rauenesfen propinquiores terre de Heuiside quam habent ex donatione me . Quare uolo ut antedicti Monachi
de Melros teneant et possideant prenominatam terram liberam . quietam . et solutam ab omni terreno serui
cio et consuetudine et exactione seculari . sicut plenius carta predicti Gaufridi testatur et confirmat . Et ego et here
des mei . manutenebimus et warantizabimus sepedictis Monachis prescriptam terram contra omnes homines inperpe
tuum . Saluo tamen seruicio mihi et heredibus meis de ipso Gaufrido et heredibus suis de eadem terra . Hiis Testibus
Roberto archidiacono . Glasguensi Iohanne Decano de Rokesburc . Ricardo nono . Waltero filio meo et herede . Radulfo de Ridale .
Roberto de Bernoldebi . Adam de Wittun et Mulits aliis .

p.cl: To all sons of Holy Mother Church present and future, Patrick of Ryedale sends greeting.

Know me to have licensed and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and monks serving God in that place the donation of Geoffrey son of Waldef of Lilliesleaf which he made to the aforesaid church and monks in respect of four oxgangs of arable land in the territory of Whitton, in free, pure, and perpetual alms, just as they lie in one holding above ‘Raven’s Fen’ near the land of ‘Heuiside’ which they have from my donation. Wherefore I will that the aforesaid monks of Melrose should hold and possess the aforenamed land free, quit, and loosed from all earthly service and custom and secular exaction, just as the charter of the said Geoffrey more fully bears witness and affirms. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant to the oft-mentioned monks the aforewritten land against all men for ever, saving only the service to me and my heirs from Geoffrey himself and his heirs in respect of the the same land.
